name: Casar Reservoir Loop
name_original: Cañada Real Soriana Occidental, Ermita de la Virgen del Prado – circular desde Casar de Cáceres
description: Moderate 22.5-mile mountain bike loop near Casar de Cáceres, featuring historic Cañada Real paths and rural Spanish scenery.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Casar De Cáceres offers routes through a landscape characterized by open plains, rolling hills, and the presence of the Casar Reservoir. The region features a mix of agricultural land and natural areas, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Elevation gains are generally moderate, making many routes accessible while still offering engaging rides. The trails often follow historical paths and rural tracks, connecting small towns and natural points of interest. Best mountain bike trails around Casar De Cáceres The most popular mountain bike trail is Circunvalación del Pantano del Casar Trail, an 11.5 miles (18.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 22 minutes to complete. This easy route circles the Casar Reservoir, offering views of the water and surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Casar Reservoir Loop, a moderate 22.5 miles (36.2 km) path. This route explores the reservoir's perimeter with varied terrain and open views. Local mountain bikers also love the Museo Vostell, Aliseda, Arroyo de Luz – circular desde la estación de tren Arroyo-Malpartida, a 37.1 miles (59.7 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and connecting local towns, often completed in about 4 hours 39 minutes. Mountain biking around Casar De Cáceres is defined by reservoir paths, open plains, and historical drover's roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy mountain bike trails to more challenging routes. The routes in Casar De Cáceres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 1900 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Casar De Cáceres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Casar De Cáceres?
A: There are over 180 mountain bike trails around Casar De Cáceres, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Casar De Cáceres?
A: The terrain around Casar De Cáceres is characterized by open plains, rolling hills, and routes around the Casar Reservoir. You'll find a mix of agricultural land and natural areas, with trails often following historical paths and rural tracks.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Casar De Cáceres?
A: Yes, there are 21 easy mountain bike trails in the area. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014464453/circunvalacion-del-pantano-del-casar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circunvalación del Pantano del Casar Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an 11.5-mile route that circles the reservoir with moderate elevation changes.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders, there are 30 difficult mountain bike trails. One challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40379610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada Real del Casar – Nice graffiti loop from Casar de Cáceres&lt;/a&gt;, which is 29.2 km long and features more significant elevation changes.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or attractions I can see while mountain biking?
A: Many routes offer views of the Casar Reservoir. Additionally, you can explore historical sites nearby. Consider routes that pass close to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4755551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Centre of Cáceres&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5674973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Bujaco&lt;/a&gt;, which are rich in history and architecture.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that are circular or loop routes?
A: Yes, many trails are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016754603/casar-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casar Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 22.5-mile route exploring the reservoir's perimeter. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21954114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bujío loop from Casar de Cáceres&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Casar De Cáceres?
A: The mountain bike routes in Casar De Cáceres are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 130 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain and the scenic views of the reservoir and surrounding landscapes.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that connect different towns or villages?
A: Yes, some routes connect local towns and villages, often following historical drover's roads. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016962906/museo-vostell-aliseda-arroyo-de-luz-circular-desde-la-estacion-de-tren-arroyo-malpartida" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Museo Vostell, Aliseda, Arroyo de Luz – circular desde la estación de tren Arroyo-Malpartida&lt;/a&gt; is a 37.1-mile route that leads through rural landscapes and connects several local towns.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike rides in this region?
A: Ride durations vary significantly based on the trail length and your pace. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014464453/circunvalacion-del-pantano-del-casar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circunvalación del Pantano del Casar Trail&lt;/a&gt; can take around 1 hour 22 minutes, while longer moderate routes might take 2-3 hours, and more extensive rides up to 4-5 hours.

Q: Are there any specific historical sites or cultural landmarks accessible by bike?
A: While mountain biking, you can find routes that pass near significant historical sites. The region is close to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4084226" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cáceres Old Town (UNESCO World Heritage Site)&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into the area's rich past. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6222082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palace and Tower of the Storks&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the best times of year for mountain biking in Casar De Cáceres?
A: The region generally experiences mild winters and hot summers. Spring and autumn are often ideal for mountain biking, offering pleasant temperatures and comfortable riding conditions. It's always advisable to check local weather forecasts before heading out.
tips: Another interesting site in Casar de Cáceres, the Hermitage of San Benito y San Blas was built between the end of the 15th century and the 18th century.

La Cañada Real Soriana Occidental is a path that runs 700 km diagonally through the center of the Iberian Peninsula. This section is perfect for gravel or mountain biking and is fast and fun with spectacular views of the surrounding prairie.
The Hermitage of Nuestra Señora del Prado is located about eight kilometers from the town, in a place known as "Viñas de la Jara" where, as in Arroyo de la Luz, there are numerous rock tombs.
Casar de Cáceres has several interesting places to see. For example, the Laguna de Casar, the Cheese Museum, and the bus station, which is architecturally very interesting.

faqs.question: How challenging is the mountain bike route from Casar de Cáceres?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a moderate mountain bike ride. It requires good fitness and advanced riding skills due to sections with uneven terrain and loose stones. While some parts of the broader Cañada Real Soriana Occidental can be technical, this circular route is generally considered moderate.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, featuring a mix of trails, forest paths, and rural roads. You should be prepared for uneven surfaces and loose stones. The route has moderate elevation changes, offering a good balance of climbs and descents.

faqs.question: What historical and natural sights can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route is part of the historic Cañada Real Soriana Occidental, an ancient transhumance path. You'll pass by the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Meadow and the Hermitage of San Benito and San Blas. The scenery includes woodlands, grasslands, and areas with holm oaks, often providing traditional Spanish rural charm.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the trailhead are not provided, Casar de Cáceres is the starting point. You might find parking options near the Casar de Cáceres Bus Station or other public areas within the town.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dogs specifically on this route is not available. However, as a general rule for mountain biking trails in natural areas, if dogs are permitted, they should always be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: The best seasons for mountain biking in this region are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, making rides less comfortable, especially during midday. Winter might bring cooler temperatures but generally remains rideable.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that permits or entrance fees are required to ride this section of the Cañada Real Soriana Occidental. These historical paths are generally open for public use.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other paths. Notably, it includes parts of the Mtb Vía de la Plata, Vía de la Plata hiking - 02 Merida to Salamanca, and the Circunvalación del pantano. You'll also find it intersecting with the Cañada Real de Cáceres and other local trails like Camino de la Vía and Cañada Real del Casar.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs near the trail?
faqs.answer: Since the route starts and ends in Casar de Cáceres, you will find various facilities such as cafes, pubs, and possibly accommodation options within the town before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Is this a good route for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its classification as a moderate mountain bike route requiring good fitness and advanced riding skills, it might not be ideal for beginners or young children. More experienced riders will likely enjoy the varied terrain and historical context.

faqs.question: What makes the Cañada Real Soriana Occidental significant?
faqs.answer: The Cañada Real Soriana Occidental is one of Spain's ancient transhumance paths, historically used for moving livestock. Riding this route offers a unique opportunity to experience centuries of pastoral heritage and enjoy the diverse natural beauty of the region.
